test.notes: Full suite exited with only the 2 known pre-existing failures documented in AGENTS.md: aiOrchestratorService single-lane integration closing strategy and orchestratorService unified-attempt laneId handling. No additional failures were observed (111 passed files, 1206 passing tests, 2 known failures).

lint.notes: Lint is not configured in the project manifest or services.yaml; the documented placeholder command completed successfully.

blockingIssues.description: Adaptive execution is not wired into the production runtime: run launch still derives parallelism from planner metadata, while the new task-complexity helpers are only exercised in tests. VAL-ENH-002 and VAL-ENH-003 are therefore not enforced at runtime.

Multi-round planning loops remain blocked because `loopTarget` is not wired into phase transitions and `spawn_worker` refuses a second planning worker after the first planning success, leaving VAL-PLAN-006 unmet.

Phase-level `agent-browser` capabilities are not propagated into spawned worker runtime metadata, so enabling `capabilities: ["agent-browser"]` on a phase has no runtime effect. VAL-ENH-050, VAL-ENH-052, and the runtime portion of VAL-CROSS-002 remain unmet.

Worker-reported screenshot/video artifacts are not preserved and matched through the artifact pipeline used by closeout checks, so `screenshot` and `browser_verification` requirements can still be missed. VAL-ENH-061, VAL-ENH-062, VAL-ENH-063, and VAL-CROSS-002 remain unmet.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented kvDb fixture-seeding schema-name quirks in `.factory/library/architecture.md` so service tests use the actual SQLite column names (`display_name`, `step_index`, `executor_kind`) instead of camelCase runtime field names.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Tighten procedure compliance rules so workers must record deviations whenever they skip required startup reads (such as `.factory/library/architecture.md`) or skip the mandated red-phase/TDD order, instead of still reporting `followedProcedure: true`.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both enhanced-orchestration service-worker features reported `followedProcedure: true` despite reviewer evidence that one skipped the required architecture-library read and the other appears to have implemented production code before creating the first failing test file.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Strengthen verification guidance to require at least one test per validation-contract area that exercises the real runtime path, not just type-level checks or direct database inserts.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The scrutiny reviews found shallow tests in both features: adaptive-runtime coverage asserted helper outputs without proving the production fan-out/planning loop path, and artifact/browser coverage relied on plain objects or manual DB inserts while the real worker-metadata and artifact pipeline remained broken.
